title: Nursing auxiliaries and assistants
description: Assess, treat, and care for patients with breathing disorders. Assume primary responsibility for all respiratory care modalities, including the supervision of respiratory therapy technicians. Initiate and conduct therapeutic procedures; maintain patient records; and select, assemble, check, and operate equipment. Diagnose, treat, and prevent disorders by stimulating specific acupuncture points within the body using acupuncture needles. May also use cups, nutritional supplements, therapeutic massage, acupressure, and other alternative health therapies. All healthcare diagnosing or treating practitioners not listed separately. (Based on 36 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Assist patients with daily living activities, such as bathing, dressing, grooming, and using supportive devices like braces or slings. Provide emotional support and encourage patients in their therapy.
Monitor and record patients' vital signs, physical symptoms, and treatment responses, reporting any changes to nursing or medical staff.
Transport patients to and from treatment areas using wheelchairs or stretchers while ensuring their safety and comfort during transfers.
Maintain a clean and organized work environment, including sanitizing equipment, cleaning treatment areas, and changing bed linens.

Assist healthcare staff with various duties, coordinate patient appointments, and manage inventory of medical supplies and medications. Participate in administrative tasks and maintain treatment records.
Participate in recreational and social activities with patients to promote engagement and well-being.
Provide basic patient care, including administering prescribed treatments and medications under supervision, and assist with mobility and exercise routines.
Support therapists and supervisors in implementing therapy plans and evaluating patient progress, including discussing treatment modifications as necessary.
Undertake weight management and dietary assessments to ensure appropriate nutrition for patients, while assisting with food preparation and serving.
Provide observations and insights regarding patients' emotional and psychological needs, facilitating communication with family and healthcare professionals.
Assess patients' physical and emotional conditions through personal interaction and monitoring, and apply therapeutic techniques as directed by healthcare providers.
Engage in constant learning and improvement of skills through attending in-service programs and continuing education opportunities.
Assist patients during rehabilitation activities by guiding them through exercises and providing necessary physical support and encouragement.
Secure patients into therapy equipment safely and assist with their needs throughout various treatment modalities.
Transport patients to and from treatment areas using wheelchairs or providing standing support.
Secure patients into or onto therapy equipment and instruct, motivate, safeguard, or assist patients practicing exercises or functional activities, under the direction of medical staff.
Observe patients during treatment to compile and evaluate data on patients' responses and progress, report findings to medical staff, and record this information in client records.
Observe and monitor patient food intake, body weight, and report changes in dietary problems to dieticians or other medical staff.
Maintain and clean equipment, work areas, or shelves, and restock storage areas, replenishing items on shelves as needed and ensuring sterilization of all instruments and supplies.
Collect, transport, and process specimens, such as blood samples, ensuring proper documentation from collection sites to medical laboratories.
Assist in the collection and transportation of hazardous waste in compliance with applicable laws, and maintain biohazard standards.
Conduct various tests, including blood tests and diagnostic imaging, and document all pertinent information regarding the measurements and results.
Administer medications and provide emergency care, including basic life support techniques such as artificial respiration and assistance with resuscitation.
Supervise other healthcare staff or assist in training new employees on procedures related to patient care and follow-up compliance with treatment plans.
Coordinate communication among patients, family members, and medical staff, including addressing inquiries or complaints and educating patients about care plans and conditions.
Respond to emergency situations, such as emergency medical calls, and manage patient care in collaboration with a team of healthcare professionals.
Attend and participate in interdisciplinary meetings to discuss patient care and maintain currency in nursing practice through ongoing education and professional development.
Provide support to patients in daily living activities, such as getting out of bed, bathing, dressing, and using the toilet, and assist with more intensive personal care needs.
Maintain knowledge of community services and healthcare resources available to patients, including insurance and patient support services.
Perform clerical tasks, such as maintaining medical records, filing, and scheduling appointments.
Conduct nutritional assessments and develop appropriate programs based on dietary history to aid in patient recovery and overall health.
Transport patients and their belongings between departments or treatment units while ensuring compliance with safety standards.
Deliver medication to treatment units and ensure accurate client information is gathered before administering prescriptions.
Document patient interactions, maintain healthcare records, and ensure compliance with confidentiality standards.
Read current literature, engage with peers, and seek further education to keep up with best practices and advancements in patient care.
Collect feedback on patient encounters and outcomes, providing recommendations for changes in care as necessary.

Operate, assemble, adjust, or monitor respiratory therapy equipment, ensuring it functions safely and efficiently. Maintain charts containing patients' pertinent information related to therapy. Monitor patients' performance in therapy activities and support them in performing tasks related to their care. Educate patients and their families on their conditions and on appropriate management techniques, including breathing exercises or the proper use of respiratory equipment. Supervise or train students or respiratory therapy technicians in these procedures.

Assist patients with daily living tasks such as dressing, grooming, bathing, and maintaining cleanliness. Monitor patients' physical and emotional well-being, reporting any unusual behavior or physical conditions to medical staff. Encourage patients to develop work skills and engage in social or recreational activities that enhance interpersonal skills and relationships. Teach patients and families about home programs and basic living skills, as well as the care and use of adaptive equipment.

Perform clerical tasks, such as scheduling appointments, collecting patient data, documenting health information, and maintaining records. Ensure proper maintenance of respiratory and other medical equipment by conducting inspections and repairs as necessary. Assist in training other healthcare staff and contribute to research or conferences as part of the healthcare team.

Gather and document patient medical histories, identification, and test results. Assess patients' vital signs and physical conditions to inform treatment plans. Follow prescribed protocols to monitor and adjust treatment based on patient responses, consulting with physicians as needed. Enforce safety protocols and ensure adherence to treatment plans as directed by medical staff.

Assist in therapeutic procedures such as respiratory care and rehabilitative practices. Explain treatment procedures to reduce patient anxiety and gain cooperation. You may assist in administering medications or injections following healthcare guidelines and physician directives, while maintaining a sterile environment during relevant procedures and monitoring patients during treatment or surgical interventions.

Administer treatments or therapies, including hydrotherapy, acupuncture, acupressure, and therapeutic exercise, to alleviate health conditions and enhance patient well-being. This includes obtaining medical histories, performing physical exams, documenting symptoms, and developing individualized care plans.

Provide care for mothers and newborns before, during, and after birth, including monitoring health progress, providing comfort measures, and ensuring safety conditions are met. This includes conducting prenatal assessments, caregiving interventions, and post-partum evaluations.

Conduct assessments and treatments related to patient health, including collecting specimens, performing diagnostic tests, and evaluating treatment outcomes. Maintain accurate medical records and collaborate with other health professionals to ensure comprehensive patient care.

Support patients through health education, counseling, and community resource referral. Provide information on topics like nutrition, pregnancy, wellness, and alternative therapies to enhance patient understanding and foster better healthcare management.
Review files, records, and other documents to obtain information to respond to requests and compile, copy, sort, and file records of office activities, business transactions, and other activities.

Operate office machines, such as photocopiers, scanners, facsimile machines, voice mail systems, and personal computers; compute, record, and proofread data and other information, such as records or reports; and open, sort, and route incoming mail, answer correspondence, and prepare outgoing mail.

Complete work schedules, manage calendars, and arrange appointments; maintain knowledge of all organizational and governmental rules affecting purchases, and provide this information to organization staff and vendors.
Approve and pay bills; complete and mail bills, contracts, policies, invoices, or checks.
Type, format, proofread, and edit correspondence and other documents from notes or dictating machines, using computers or typewriters.
Train and supervise subordinates and monitor and direct the work of lower-level clerks.
Deliver messages and run errands; inventory and order materials, supplies, and services.
Process and prepare documents, such as business or government forms and expense reports.

emerging_tasks.task: Confirm the identities of patients by verifying their personal information.
Order medical supplies for healthcare facilities or laboratories.
Attend high-risk and caesarian section infant deliveries to provide neonatal respiratory care as needed.
Perform general administrative tasks, such as answering telephones, scheduling appointments, or ordering supplies or equipment.
Transport patients to and from the operating room.
Test patients for sexually transmitted infections.
